{{Quote|Lando's kind of... special... to me. I might lose a little sleep if I let you take him.|Drea Renthal to [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]]|<ref name="Rebel Dawn">''[[Rebel Dawn]]''</ref>}}
'''Drea Renthal''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who served as commander of a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] fleet in the years prior to the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Drea was not typical of other pirates since she had a code of ethics that tried to minimize casualties. A self-styled businesswoman, she was only in the game for quick credits and would never lower her standards to deal in [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]]. She was known to take hostages but always returned the hostage unharmed when the ransom was paid.

==Biography==
===Anatomy of a pirate queen===
Approximately 46 standard years old in the time of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Drea Renthal was a big woman with squarish features and a strong alto voice. She always dressed in mismatched clothes, wearing garments that clashed with each other, along with various sets of armor. Her hair, done up short and spiky, was silver and gold. Her typical style of headwear included a flamboyant beret with a trailing orange feather. Though always dressed in outrageous fashion and a dangerous warrior to boot, people who knew her thought of her as extremely refined and intelligent.

===The [[Battle of Nar Shaddaa (4 BBY)|Battle of Nar Shaddaa]]===
[[File:Drea_Renthal.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Drea Renthal in her Y-wing]]
In [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]] hired Drea Renthal to protect [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]] from an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] fleet that was going to make an example of the criminal moon of [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]] by enacting [[Base Delta Zero/Legends|Base Delta Zero]]. Though she had no orders to engage the Empire to protect the moon, she decided it was in her best interests to join the smuggler fleet and attack the Empire en masse when Admiral [[Winstel Greelanx|Greelanx]] arrived with the fleet. Her orders were to protect the Hutts, and although Greelanx had been paid off to leave Nal Hutta alone, there was still a chance that the Empire would attack Hutt interests on Nal Hutta. Because her fleet consisted mostly of larger capital ships and squadrons of [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wings]], she was instrumental to the smugglers by getting Greelanx to order a retreat. Her many accomplishments during the [[Battle of Nar Shaddaa (4 BBY)|Battle of Nar Shaddaa]] included the crippling of the ''[[Renthal's Vigilance]]'' and the destruction of the [[Arrestor (Neutron Star-class)|''Arrestor'']] and [[Peacekeeper (Dreadnaught-class)|''Peacekeeper'']]. Afterward, Drea salvaged the ''Vigilance'' and brought the [[Carrack-class light cruiser/Legends|Carrack]] into her own fleet, thereby increasing her firepower and her ability to make a quick credit. She rechristened the new ship ''[[Renthal's Vigilance]]''.

===Gambling with a love affair===
For a short time afterwards she became lover of [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], even though she was "old enough to be his mother," as [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] said when teasing Lando about his relationship. After Lando decided life aboard a pirate vessel was not his style, he picked up and went on his own way, breaking their relationship but remaining good friends. After their relationship ended, Drea dropped Lando off at [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] for a gambling tournament that changed his and Han Solo's lives forever, for this was the event where Han won from Lando the famous [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']].

===Pirate queen versus ''Queen of Empire''===
In [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]] Drea Renthal encountered Lando Calrissian again a year down the road at what would be a most opportune time for a second meeting. While Lando embarked aboard the ''[[Queen of Empire]]'' for a week of gambling, he ran afoul of [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] while turning his infamous charm on Fett's bounty, a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] named [[Bria Tharen]]. But as Lando assumed correctly, Drea Renthal targeted the luxury liner for a raid and dragged a large asteroid through the ship's [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] route to tug the ''Queen'' back into realspace. When her pirates raided the vessel, Drea and her mercenaries cornered Fett and forced him to let go both Lando (as her ex-lover) and Bria (who owed debt to Han Solo from the Battle of Nar Shaddaa), going so far as to pay him a hundred-thousand-credit bounty using credits stolen from passengers of the luxury liner. She did that good deed simply because she was still fond of the young gambler.
